title: NOC 92020 – Supervisors, motor vehicle assembling | GTR Immigration
description: Supervisors under NOC 92020 are the operational heartbeat of Canada’s automotive manufacturing sector. As a TEER 2 occupation, these professionals coordinate the complex assembly lines that produce automobiles, vans, and light trucks. In 2026, as Canada cements its position as a global "EV Hub" with massive investments in electric vehicle plants across Southern Ontario, these supervisors are in record-high demand. For immigration, this role is a premier candidate for the OINP Employer Job Offer stream and the New 2026 TR to PR Pathway.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Level2Major Group92 – Processing, manufacturing and utilities supervisorsSub-Major Group920 – Processing, manufacturing and utilities supervisorsMinor Group9202 – Supervisors, assembly and fabricationNOC Code92020

common_job_titles: Body Shop Supervisor - Motor Vehicle Assembly
Paint Shop Foreman/Woman - Motor Vehicle Manufacturing
duties: Supervision: Coordinate and schedule the activities of workers in production departments such as body shop, chassis, paint, trim, and final assembly.
Operational Strategy: Establish methods to meet strict work schedules and coordinate activities with other departments (Logistics, Engineering).
Problem Solving: Resolve technical work problems and recommend measures to improve productivity and vehicle build quality.
Staff Development: Train staff in specific job duties, advanced safety procedures for EV handling, and company policies.
Resource Management: Requisition materials and supplies; recommend personnel actions such as hirings and promotions.
Reporting: Prepare detailed production logs, safety audit reports, and quality assurance summaries for plant management.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is mandatory.
Education: Post-secondary education in engineering, sciences, or business is usually required for modern supervisory roles.
Work Experience: Several years of experience as a production worker (Assembler/Inspector) in motor vehicle manufacturing are mandatory.
Technical Skills: Proficiency in Lean Manufacturing (Six Sigma), ISO standards, and manufacturing execution systems (MES).
Strong leadership, time management, and the ability to work in high-pressure, fast-paced environments are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, total annual earnings for senior supervisors in Ontario’s EV sector often range from $85,000 to $115,000 inclusive of overtime and performance bonuses.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. According to the Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS), the automotive sector is undergoing its largest transformation in a century. The retirement of the "Boomer" generation of plant foremen, combined with the opening of several new multi-billion dollar battery and assembly plants, ensures that qualified supervisors will have significant bargaining power through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The demand for motor vehicle assembly supervisors is Very Good, particularly in Canada’s "Automotive Corridor." In 2026, Ontario (Windsor, Oakville, Oshawa) is the primary hiring hub, followed by emerging EV clusters in Quebec. With major manufacturers transitioning to electric platforms, there is a specific shortage of supervisors who understand high-voltage battery integration and automated robotic assembly. National projections suggest a sustained need for over 1,500 new supervisors by 2027 to meet industry expansion.
EV Revolution Leadership: Be at the forefront of the global shift to electric mobility by managing state-of-the-art EV assembly lines.
Industry 4.0 Integration: Gain experience with AI-driven production scheduling, collaborative robots (cobots), and digital twin technology.
Premium Benefits: Over 98% of workers in this NOC in Ontario receive comprehensive non-wage benefits, including dental, medical, and pension plans.
Strategic PR Pathways: This TEER 2 role is highly prioritized in Express Entry and provincial "In-Demand" manufacturing streams.
pathways: New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A temporary measure targeting 33,000 workers already in Canada, specifically in essential manufacturing and skilled trades.
OINP Employer Job Offer (Foreign Worker Stream): A direct route for those with a job offer from a motor vehicle manufacturer in Ontario.
Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Potential targeting under the "Trade" or "STEM" categories for candidates with process engineering backgrounds.
BC PNP Skills Immigration: Priority for supervisors supporting the province’s growing specialized vehicle and components manufacturing sector.
